params.solver: Identifier to set as the solver.

params.output: Output description of the order to dispute.

efficientRequireProven(bytes).details: More efficient implementation of isProven. Does not return a boolean, instead reverts if false. This function returns true if proofSeries is empty.

params.proofSeries: remoteChainId, remoteOracle, application, and dataHash encoded in chucks of 32*4=128 bytes.

params.output: Output description of the order to dispute.

hasAttested(bytes[]).details: Allows oracles to verify we have confirmed payloads.

params.application: Application that the data originated from.
params.remoteOracle: Attestor on the data origin chain.

returns._0: bool Whether the hashed data has been attested to.

params.output: Output description of the order to dispute.

params.blockNum: Bitcoin block number of block that included the transaction that fills the output.
params.inclusionProof: Context required to validate an output has been filled.
params.output: Output to prove has been filled.
params.txOutIndex: Index of the output in the transaction being proved.

verify(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,.details: This function technically extends the verification of outputs 1 block (~10 minutes) into the past beyond what _validateTimestamp would ordinary allow. The purpose is to protect against slow block mining. Even if it took days to mine 1 block for a transaction, it would still be possible to include the proof with a valid time. (assuming the oracle period isn't over yet).

params.blockNum: Bitcoin block number of block that included the transaction that fills the output.
params.inclusionProof: Context required to validate an output has been filled.
params.output: Output to prove has been filled.
params.previousBlockHeader: Header of the block before blockNum. Timestamp will be collected from this header.
params.txOutIndex: Index of the output in the transaction being proved.

CHALLENGER_COLLATERAL_FACTOR().notice: Require that the challenger provides X times the collateral of the claimant.

DISPUTED_ORDER_FEE_DESTINATION().notice: The purpose of the dispute fee is to make sure that 1 person can't claim and dispute the transaction at no risk.

efficientRequireProven(bytes).notice: Check if a series of data has been attested to.

finaliseDispute(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes32.notice: Finalise a dispute if the order hasn't been proven.

isProven(uint256,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32.notice: Check if some data has been attested to on some chain.

optimisticallyVerify(bytes32,(bytes32,by.notice: Optimistically verify an order if the order has not been disputed.

verify(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,.notice: Validate an output has been included in a block with appropriate confirmation.
Validate an output has been included in a block with appropriate confirmation using a timestamp from a older block.
